title: gbrain #485: check-backlinks positional dir parsing
summary: For gbrain issue 485 / PR 825, check backlinks supports documented positional dir for both check and fix. The CLI previously only honored dir and otherwise scanned '.', so running from a parent directory could walk unrel
body:
For gbrain issue #485 / PR #825, check-backlinks supports documented positional [dir] for both check and fix. The CLI previously only honored --dir and otherwise scanned '.', so running from a parent directory could walk unrelated siblings. Keep parsing in parseBacklinksArgs: --dir wins over positional, --dry-run is not treated as a path, and default remains '.'.
Evidence: Issue https://github.com/garrytan/gbrain/issues/485 and PR https://github.com/garrytan/gbrain/pull/825. Added parser tests in test/backlinks.test.ts. Tests passed: bun test test/backlinks.test.ts, 14 pass 0 fail; bun run typecheck and git diff --check passed.
Verified by: test/backlinks.test.ts, bun run typecheck, git diff --check
